$schema_note: Pass-2 harvest by MisconceptionAnalyst from common_miss in data/buckets/verb_form.future.json. PENDING ratification. Uses families ratified in thread v2; new specifics defined here, reused ones under reuses_existing. Thread: inter_chat/Architecture_MisconceptionAnalyst_registry_harvest.md.

proposed_family_actions.note: Future/conditional stem changes (the -are→-er shift and syncope) are conditioned stem changes; they fit stem_change's over/under shape.
See pronoun file and v3 for the gemination family proposal; the syncopated-future double-r lands here.

proposed_misconceptions.proposed_description: Failing to shift the -are theme vowel to -er- in the future/conditional stem, or over-applying that shift to verbs (dare/stare/fare) that keep -ar-.
proposed_misconceptions.cross_kind_note: Cross-tense: identical in the conditional (parlarei, derei).
proposed_misconceptions.rationale: Both directions of the same conditioned shift.

proposed_misconceptions.proposed_description: Syncopating a regular stem by false analogy with the irregular syncopated futures.
proposed_misconceptions.rationale: Mirrors the present-tense over/under stem-change pairs.

proposed_misconceptions.proposed_description: Keeping the theme vowel where the irregular future/conditional stem syncopates (avr-, andr-, potr-).

Writing a single consonant where Italian has a phonemic geminate: the assimilated -rr- of syncopated futures/conditionals, the -ss- of the imperfect subjunctive, the -mm-/-mm of passato-remoto/conditional 1pl.
proposed_misconceptions.cross_kind_note: New tentative gemination family (v2 E moved gemination out of orthography). Cross-kind: also conditional vorei, congiuntivo parlasi, passato_remoto parlamo, and the enclitic dammi.
proposed_misconceptions.rationale: Geminates are phonemic, so single-spelling is a sound error, not sound-preserving spelling.

reuses_existing.defined_in: data/misconceptions.json (v1 seed) — generalised per v3 decision C

reuses_existing.note: cercerò: same hard-c/g h-rule, in the future stem.
Dropping the obligatory 1sg/3sg future accent (parlerò, sarà). Heavy cross-tense reuse of the existing accent specific.
esserò/essrò: building from the infinitive instead of the suppletive sar- stem.
sarò andato for a feminine subject (andata).
